item.title: Aberrant p53 overexpression in benign colon biopsies may predict dysplasia risk in patients with primary sclerosing cholangitis and inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Histopathology - Patients with primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), collectively termed PSC-IBD, have an increased risk of developing nonconventional and/or invisible colorecta...

item.title: HIF-1α in macrophage polarization: roles in immunometabolism and autoimmune diseases.
item.description: Journal of leukocyte biology - Hypoxia-inducible factor-1α (HIF-1α) is a central regulator of cellular responses to hypoxia and plays a pivotal role in immune cell activation and functional reprogramming. This review summarizes ...

item.title: [A potpourri of diagnoses-Unusual manifestations of a chronic inflammatory bowel disease : Case report].
item.description: Zeitschrift fur Rheumatologie - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) are frequently associated with extraintestinal manifestations, which usually occur concomitantly with or following onset of an intestinal disease. The presence of ...

item.title: Bimekizumab Long-Term Safety and Efficacy Across the Spectrum of Axial Spondyloarthritis Over 3 Years: Results from Two Phase 3 Studies.
item.description: The Journal of rheumatology - 2-year safety and efficacy of bimekizumab, a dual IL-17A and IL-17F inhibitor, has been demonstrated across the full disease spectrum of axial spondyloarthritis (axSpA). We report bimekizumab long-...

item.title: Tracheobronchial stenosis as a rare extraintestinal manifestation of ulcerativecolitis: a case series with different therapeutic approaches.
item.description: Frontiers in medicine - Case report - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease characterized by extraintestinal manifestations in nearly half of patients. Although pulmonary involvement has traditionally been con...

item.title: Multi-layered functional genomics prioritizes candidate effectors and regulatory mechanisms of ankylosing spondylitis.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Meta-analysis - Ankylosing spondylitis (AS) is a chronic inflammatory arthropathy with heritability estimated at approximately 90%, yet the effector genes and regulatory mechanisms beyond the well-established HLA-...
